text:
BEHOLD! AI Frens, my first wholly-original prompt for your #POTD pleasure! Built with the help of @EvaGlitchAI and the #AbyssalSaloon (@DelDemonOver9k @RaynTarant @Dyabolicalone @AizenAsahiXIII @KaishiAsahi @gojirazard963)

It's supposed to get weird. Just go with it. 
PROMPT WASH
------------------------------------------------------------
Use the attached character reference image as the sole identity source for the character.
All design decisions must originate from this reference.

The character is entering a fictional transformation facility known as the Prompt Wash, a retro-futuristic machine that radically reinterprets characters while preserving their core identity.

CORE IDENTITY ANCHOR (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
The final character must remain clearly recognizable as the same individual from the reference image.

Preserve:
- Core silhouette and proportions
- physical attributes like animal ears, wings, or tail
- Signature colors and value structure
- Iconic accessories and props
- Distinctive markings or facial features
- Overall personality, attitude, and visual “vibe”
The redesign must feel like a continuation of identity, not a replacement.

Target transformation balance:
20–30% original identity preserved
70–80% creatively reimagined

CHARACTER TRANSFORMATION RULES
Each generation MUST randomly select:
A. 1 primary world genre (mandatory):
surrealist painting world
fantasy illustration world
vintage advertising world
children's storybook world
industrial documentary realism
ancient mythological world
high-fashion editorial world
brutalist architecture world
pastoral countryside world
abstract geometric world

B. 1–2 secondary influences (optional blending):
fashion eras
materials
animal traits
cultural motifs
occupational archetypes
environmental physics (fog, ink, glass, clay, paper, water, sand, etc.)

C. 0–1 sci-fi element ONLY (optional)
If used, it must be subtle (not architectural dominance)

These influences must:
Feel visually coherent together
Be fused into a single unified redesign
Compliment the character’s identity

Do NOT explicitly reveal or label the influences in the final image.

CHARACTER STYLE SOURCE LOCK
Character redesign MUST be derived exclusively from:
reference image identity
randomly selected transformation influences

The facility environment is explicitly excluded as a style reference.

Do NOT infer:
neon/cyberpunk aesthetics from lighting
sci-fi themes from architecture
futuristic styling from UI elements

Lighting and environment are neutral rendering conditions, not aesthetic directives.

SCENE STRUCTURE (21:9 ULTRA-WIDE CINEMATIC)
Create a split composition, do not label each side, ensure visible break between two images:

LEFT SIDE — ENTRY STATE (BEFORE TRANSFORMATION)
Show the original character approaching the Prompt Wash entrance tunnel
- The character is seen from behind and slightly to the side
- The character is actively walking forward into the Prompt Wash entrance
- The viewer is positioned behind the character, following their movement into the facility
- The character design remains unchanged except for staging and lighting
Include a visible UI-style selection screen showing transformation options being applied
Perspective: elevated, angled three-quarter cinematic view
Environment is clean, readable, and clearly pre-transformation

RIGHT SIDE — EXIT STATE (AFTER TRANSFORMATION)
Show the transformed character standing just outside the exit tunnel
the character is facing the viewer
The transformation has just completed moments ago
The character is the primary focal point

PROMPT WASH FACILITY DESIGN
The facility is SCENOGRAPHY ONLY and must not define character aesthetic direction.
The Prompt Wash facility design is fixed and non-influential across all generations.

It must remain visually consistent:
retro-futuristic touchless wash architecture
mid-century roadside attraction structure
neon signage and UI elements
wet reflective flooring
tunnel-based entrance/exit system
Maintain strong visual continuity between entrance and exit sides.

IMPORTANT CONSTRAINT:
The facility is purely environmental set dressing and MUST NOT influence:
character styling decisions
aesthetic genre of transformation
fashion or material choices
color palette evolution
visual era of character redesign

The character transformation must originate ONLY from the character reference image + transformation rules.
The facility is a static “stage,” not a design influence system.

LEFT SIDE ENVIRONMENT (ENTRY)
Include:
Illuminated marquee sign: “TOUCHLESS PROMPT WASH”
Tagline: “CLEAN. REFRESH. YOU.”
Neon retro-future kiosk with menu:
- Anime Rinse
- Foxu Mist
- Himbo Cleanse
- POTD Dry
- Color Selection
Foam/steam buildup near tunnel
Conveyor walkway leading inward
No visible exit tunnel

RIGHT SIDE ENVIRONMENT (EXIT)
Include:
Illuminated sign: “PROMPT WASH COMPLETE”
Secondary sign: “YOU ARE NOW REFRESHED”
Foam-coated exit tunnel
Wet pavement with strong reflections
Mist drifting outward from tunnel
Subtle environmental shifts reflecting the character’s transformation style
No visible entrance

IMPORTANT - The Left and Right side facility design must be cohesive. 

TRANSFORMATION DESIGN SYSTEM
The character may undergo changes to:
Style language (fashion, era, aesthetic)
Materials and surface textures
Anatomy proportions (subtle to moderate only)
Species or hybrid traits (if coherent)
Sci-fi or fantasy augmentation
Color reinterpretation (while preserving signature anchors)
The result must feel professionally designed and intentional, not chaotic or arbitrary.
Do no let entrance menu affect transformation output.

TRANSFORMATION RESIDUE (CONTROLLED VFX)
Depict evidence that transformation just occurred.
Use a limited, controlled mix of effects:

Physical effects:
Foam residue dripping
Water droplets on surfaces
Wet footprints
Steam and mist near tunnel

Energy/digital effects (use sparingly):
Subtle glitches or scan distortions
Soft glow particles or floating motes

Important:
Do NOT overload the scene with all effects at maximum intensity
Effects must never obscure the character’s face, silhouette, or readability

CHARACTER ACTING & EXPRESSION
The character should visibly react to the transformation:
surprise, curiosity, amusement, confusion, or mild existential realization
natural, readable body language
grounded, character-driven pose
The character must remain the focal narrative anchor.

COMPOSITION & VISUAL HIERARCHY
21:9 ultra-wide cinematic composition
Split scene must remain balanced and readable
The character must be the primary focal point in both halves
Ensure strong silhouette separation from environment
Avoid clutter or competing focal points
Environment supports story but never dominates character

LIGHTING & MOOD
Bright cinematic lighting with high readability
Neon highlights and reflective wet surfaces
Atmospheric mist and soft volumetric glow
Playful, surreal, slightly absurd tone
Polished concept art presentation

QUALITY TARGET
High-end professional concept art
Clean rendering and strong shape design
Clear storytelling readability
Detailed but not noisy composition
Strong focal hierarchy and visual clarity

HARD CONSTRAINTS (DO NOT)
Do not lose character recognizability
Do not turn the character into a generic person or unrelated design
Do not apply minor “costume swap” level changes only
Do not overcrowd the composition
Do not introduce extra characters or duplicates
Do not obscure the character with effects, foam, or mist
Do not break anatomy unless clearly justified by transformation concept
Do not make the scene visually confusing or unreadable
Do not explicitly list or name the hidden influences used
Do not make either half of the composition feel dominant over the other
Do not let environment overpower the character
Do not let environment design influence character transformation selections
--------------------------------------------------------
#promptoftheday #AI #AiArt #Art #AnimeArt #promptWash #PromptEngineering #CharacterDesign #DigitalArt #AnimeStyle #CommunityPrompt #GetWeird #SCU #Skyebros #TheCampfire

The smartest AI in finance is the one you never notice.

When people hear "AI," they usually think about chatbots or image generators.

But some of the most useful AI works quietly in the background of the financial apps millions of people use every day.

Here are a few simple examples:

➡️ Your banking app automatically groups your spending into categories like groceries, transport, and shopping, making it easier to understand where your money goes.

➡️ When you deposit a check with your phone, AI can help recognize the text and process the information more quickly.

➡️ If your card is suddenly used in a different city or country, AI may flag the activity and ask you to confirm it's really you before the payment is completed.

➡️ Customer support teams also use AI to handle routine questions, allowing human agents to spend more time helping with more complex issues.

Of course, AI isn't flawless.

It can misunderstand information or make incorrect predictions, which is why human review remains an important part of financial services.

For me, that's the most interesting part.

The goal isn't to replace people. It's to make everyday financial services faster, more efficient, and in some cases, more secure.

The more you understand how AI is being used, the easier it becomes to separate real innovation from marketing hype.

Keep learning, verify information through trusted sources, and always do your own research (DYOR).

How to run a 9B vision AI model on your laptop for FREE forever 😳

qwen just open sourced qwen 3.5 9b

no credits

no API bill

no data sent to any server when you run it locally

what you get for $0:

- qwen 3.5 9b model weights
- text + image understanding
- 262,000 native context
- no daily quota & no expiry
- works with ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp and more

how to run it:

- install ollama
- open your terminal
- run this:
"ollama run qwen3.5:9b"
- wait for it to download
- start testing it with your docs, images, code, or research

what can qwen 3.5 9b do:

- read screenshots and charts
- analyse PDFs and documents
- help with code and debugging
- answer visual math questions
- run lightweight local agents
- work with long notes and research

things to keep in mind:

- the 9b version needs decent RAM
- the quantized Ollama version is around 6.6GB to download
- local speed depends on your laptop or GPU
- use the 4b or 2b version if 9b feels slow
- 1M context needs extra configuration, it is not the default
- local AI is free, but your electricity and hardware are not

> this is better than another 7-day AI trial because you can run it forever

bookmark this if you want a strong local AI setup without paying monthly API bills

A decade of travel, memories, and textures - now brought on-chain.

Created by fashion photographer and visual artist Jenya Vyguzov, the collection transforms textures captured across years of travel into handcrafted collage flowers , each petal representing a place, memory, or moment. No AI, no generative outputs, every piece is individually composed by hand.

What stands out here is the artist behind it. Jenya has spent over a decade working with brands like Vogue, GQ Style, Tatler, Elle, Puma, and IKEA, while building a distinctive visual language. His work was even selected for the official Paris Prints Textile Show poster, where he was given an entire floor for a solo exhibition.

⚠️WL applications are coming soon. If you're interested in art NFTs, this is the stage where you want to be paying attention. Follow the artist and the project, turn on notifications, and be ready when WL applications open.

🔎 Mint Details TBA

Artist: https://t.co/3fhvrJG7YE
X: https://t.co/NzBpvbQLAh
Website: https://t.co/Uvq8Rzy1F5

dyor/nfa

Diffusion language models, which generate text by repeatedly filling in masked parts rather than writing strictly from left to right, are becoming a serious alternative to standard transformer language models, but the usual ways of improving reasoning after training were built for left-to-right generation and do not fit the diffusion models.

The authors of this paper introduce a self-training method in which the model first produces its own answer, then uses parts of that completed answer as helpful context while learning how earlier denoising steps should behave.

Instead of comparing teacher and student one next token at a time, it compares them at each denoising step, which matches how diffusion language models actually generate text. 

Across math and planning tasks, d-OPSD matches or beats supervised finetuning and reinforcement-learning baselines, while needing far fewer optimization steps than the main RL baseline.

https://t.co/eRqYj4YPwi

Excited to share that RL-100 has been accepted to Science Robotics !

We have open-sourced the code here: https://t.co/CfqoZnhB5B

Beyond the paper results, RL-100 provides a unified library for diffusion/flow policy RL post-training, iterative offline data flywheels, and real-world robot RL.

A key lesson: real-world robot RL is a system problem. Calibration, rewards, rollout data flywheels, stochastic data collection, randomization, safety checks, and deployment details all matter.

Hope this repo helps make real-world robot RL more reproducible and easier to build on.
